curation.synopsis: A diary recovered from the ruins of Hive Blackbracken records the last weeks of a slaughterhouse labourer as the great hive falls to the Plague God. Page by page the entries drift from grim shift-routines and ration counts into something stranger and worse — pestilent dreams, a creeping rot at the meat-pits, neighbours who will not stop changing. Beyond the broken plascrete a green-skied garden grows where the manufactorum streets used to be, and a horned, swine-headed shape begins to walk among the surviving butchers. The diarist's small kindnesses sharpen the dread rather than soften it. Less a story of armies than of one ordinary mortal trying to put a shape to the end of his world.

flags.note: Roster marks 'novel' but King of Pigs is a Black Library eShort (~31 pp), later collected in The Resting Places anthology
